author: lOrm
content:
## Current state (Phase 1, src/memdir/ + src/services/background.rs)

**`src/memdir/` (~806 LOC):**
- `entrypoint.rs` ships MEMORY.md discovery + loading + truncation:
  - `load_entrypoint(cwd: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<Option<EntrypointFile>>` walks `cwd â†’ cwd.parent()*` looking for `MEMORY.md` / `CLAUDE.md` / `.claude/MEMORY.md` via `discovery_candidates()`.
  - `EntrypointFile { path, content, truncation: EntrypointTruncation::{Untouched|TruncatedByLines|TruncatedByBytes} }`.
  - Caps: `MAX_ENTRYPOINT_LINES = 200`, `MAX_ENTRYPOINT_BYTES = 25_000`, `utf8_safe_truncate()` keeps byte boundary on char.
- `mod.rs` re-exports the entry-point surface. **Nothing wires it into `prompt.rs` yet** â€” opt-in by design per module-level doc.

**`src/services/background.rs` (577 LOC):**
- `BackgroundJob` trait: `name() -> &'static str` + `run(&self, db: &Arc<MemoryDb>) -> Result<JobOutcome>`.
- `JobScheduler { jobs: Vec<(Arc<dyn BackgroundJob>, Duration, Instant)> }` with `register()` + synchronous `tick() -> Vec<JobOutcome>`.
- Only concrete job: `MemoryConsolidationJob` â€” prunes expired short-term + dedups archival memory by exact-content match.

**Not landed:** autoDream consolidation subagent, MagicDocs auto-docs, SessionMemory `.md` writer, PromptSuggestion speculative subagent, prompt.rs wiring of `load_entrypoint`, settings.json `lastProjectMemoryUpdate` timestamp.

## Phase 2 spec â€” concrete sub-features

**(a) Wire MEMORY.md into the system prompt â€” single-file change**
- File: `src/prompt.rs` (or `src/context.rs`).
- In `build_system_prompt(cwd)`: call `memdir::load_entrypoint(cwd)?`; if `Some(file)`, append `\n\n# Project memory\n\n{file.content}` as a cacheable block (use the existing prompt-cache marker so it doesn't bust the cache on every turn).
- Test: write a temp `MEMORY.md`, assert the built prompt contains the content + cache marker.

**(b) SessionMemory writer â€” new file `src/memdir/session_memory.rs`**
- `pub fn write_session_note(project_dir: &Path, session_id: &str, summary: &str) -> Result<PathBuf>`.
- Writes to `~/.openclaudia/projects/<project-hash>/<session-id>/session-memory/summary.md` (mirror CC's `~/.claude/projects/X/Y/session-memory/summary.md`).
- Called from `SessionManager::end_session` (src/session/mod.rs:756) after persisting `handoff.md`.
- Project hash = first 12 chars of `sha256(project_dir.canonicalize()?.as_os_str())` to keep paths short and stable.

**(c) autoDream consolidation as a `BackgroundJob`**
- New file `src/memdir/autodream.rs` implementing `BackgroundJob`.
- `run()` spawns a `subagent::run_subagent` with `AgentType::Explore` + a fixed prompt: "Read the last N session-memory/*.md files in this project and merge duplicate facts into a single consolidated note. Write the result to MEMORY.md."
- Register on `SessionManager::end_session` (one-shot tick, not periodic) gated by a `settings.lastProjectMemoryUpdate` timestamp (write to `~/.openclaudia/settings.json`).
- Test: pre-seed two session notes with overlapping facts, run job, assert MEMORY.md contains both deduped.

**(d) PromptSuggestion speculation**
- New file `src/memdir/suggest.rs`.
- `pub async fn suggest_next_prompts(session: &Session, app_config: &AppConfig, client: &Client) -> Vec<String>` â€” forks a `run_subagent` with `tools_denied: ALL` (cache-safe â€” pure read) and asks for 3 likely follow-up prompts as JSON.
- Triggered from `tui/app.rs` on prompt-input idle (200 ms debounce after last keystroke).
- The tool-denial callback already exists pattern-wise â€” extend `SubagentConfig` with `allowed_tools: Option<HashSet<&'static str>>` so `None` means default and `Some(empty)` means deny-all.

**Phase 2 file count:** 4 new files (`session_memory.rs`, `autodream.rs`, `suggest.rs`, plus a `memdir/tests/` integration test), 1 edit (`prompt.rs`), 1 edit (`session/mod.rs:756`), 1 edit (`subagent.rs` to add `allowed_tools` to `SubagentConfig`).

Sub-issues already filed: #632 autoDream, #633 MagicDocs, #634 SessionMemory, #635 PromptSuggestion. This ticket closes as the umbrella; Phase 2 work proceeds in those.
